title: Centrolepis strigosa
intro: Centrolepis strigosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Centrolepidaceae, commonly known as the needle grass. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Centrolepis strigosa include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Centrolepis strigosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Centrolepis strigosa is classified as a species of sedge in the family Centrolepidaceae. This family is a group of small, grass-like plants commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Centrolepis strigosa is further categorized as a member of the genus Centrolepis, which consists of a small number of species found in Australia and New Zealand. The plant's classification is based on its unique characteristics, such as its small size and distinctive leaf structure. As a member of the Centrolepidaceae family, Centrolepis strigosa is part of a distinct group of plants that are often found in shaded, humid environments.
